---
title: "6_fitting_to_exponential"
format: html
---

# Objective

We would like to see how the difference in fit of secondary responses to a power law vs an exponential decay function changes as a function of $\tau$ and $\delta$. 
 
Because we would like to understand whether the addition of prior responses can explain the "unreasonably" good fit of a power law vs an exponential function to antibody titer decay, we will perform this fitting in the same way as we preformed fitting to antibody titer time series a la mark slifka. 

We disregard the first three years after the boost and then fit. 

As such, we will only fit the secondary response for which there is at least 48 months of data after boost ($time - \tau > 36$), and we will disregard the first 36 months.

# Load data

We load our generated secondary responses below: 

```{r load-time-series}
# load time series
time.series = read.csv(here("Theory", "data", "secondary response", "secodnary_responses.csv"))

# 
```
